source.note: Pre-Assembly Insertion Nut for 6 Series (HFS6) extrusions. HFS6 series groove opening = 8 mm (literal catalog drawing dimension) -- NOT the HFS8 series (which is a 10 mm slot). The series numeral != slot width in mm.

part_frame.note: Local frame chosen to match the extrusion T-slot section convention so profile_containment compares apples-to-apples: u = X across the slot (width 14 -> -7..7), v = Y depth from the extrusion top face (face datum v=0, into the slot is negative), w = Z along the slot (length 15). The nut seats in the slot CAVITY just below the lips: its captured rectangle is u[-7,7] x v[-2.3,-8.6] (14 mm wide, 6.3 mm tall, top 0.1 mm below the v=-2.2 lip underside). 14 mm > the 8 mm opening => the lips capture it; 14 mm < the 16 mm cavity and 6.3 mm < the 12.2 mm cavity depth => it seats. The M5 tap runs vertically (along +Y, opening toward the slot face) so a clamp screw dropped through the rail/foot clearance hole threads down into it. CAD bbox 14.00 x 6.30 x 15.00 mm corroborates the footprint/height.

evidence.locator: HNTT6-5 detail-page dimensioned drawing, front view: footprint 14 x 15 mm, base height 6.3 mm; CAD bbox 14.00 x 6.30 x 15.00 corroborates
evidence.raw_value: front-view footprint 14 x 15 mm, base height 6.3 mm; CAD bbox 14.00 x 6.30 x 15.00

evidence.locator: HNTT6 catalog page HFS6-series groove-width icon: opening literally dimensioned 8 mm ('For HFS6 Series Aluminum Extrusions 30/50/60/100 Square'); cavity 16 / depth 12.2 from 6575N203 -> 14 wide captured by the 8 opening, fits the 16 cavity
evidence.raw_value: HFS6 series groove opening literally dimensioned 8 mm; nut 14 wide captured by 8 opening, fits 16 cavity
Thread Nominal M(mm) = M5; PN grammar HNTT6-{M} => -5 is M5

provenance.annotated_by: lead: drawing + CAD bbox + cross-checked against 6575N203 slot section
